hypothesis: The current VWAP logic is momentum-based (price above VWAP → bull score), which directly contradicts RSI mean-reversion. When RSI is oversold (<30), price has dropped below VWAP — so VWAP was adding ~21 pts to bear_score while RSI added 40 pts to bull_score, partially cancelling. Inverting VWAP to mean-reversion logic (price below VWAP → bull, price above VWAP → bear) makes RSI and VWAP reinforce each other on the best setups, boosting confidence and improving win rate / Sharpe.

hypothesis: The current RSI scoring is binary — RSI=29 and RSI=15 both score the same 40 pts. RSI < 25 (very oversold) has much stronger mean-reversion potential than RSI 25-30, as prices are at a more extreme deviation from fair value. Adding a graduated tier (RSI < 25 → 120 pts vs 100 pts for RSI 25-30, and RSI > 75 → 120 pts for overbought) differentiates signal quality without changing trade count. Higher confidence on the strongest setups should improve win rate and Sharpe — the two largest fitness components.

hypothesis: VWAP is a momentum indicator that conflicts with RSI mean-reversion. When RSI is oversold (<30), price has dropped below VWAP — VWAP adds ~21 bear pts (0.30*70) partially cancelling RSI bull signal (40 pts). Shifting 0.05 from VWAP to RSI and 0.05 from VWAP to Volume reduces this conflict: RSI extremes score higher (45 pts), VWAP bear pressure is smaller (14 pts), and volume confirmation is stronger (25 pts). Net bull on quality RSI-extreme setups rises from ~60 to ~70 raw, more consistently passing MIN_CONFIDENCE=58 and at higher confidence — improving Sharpe and Sortino.

hypothesis: The first 5-15 minutes after market open (9:30-9:45 ET) are the most volatile period of the trading day — dominated by gap fills, order imbalances, and algo noise rather than clean RSI mean-reversion setups. Currently we skip only 5 minutes (9:30-9:35). Extending to 15 minutes means signals are generated only after price has stabilized into a genuine intraday trend. Fewer but higher-quality trades should reduce losers, improve win rate, and raise Sharpe/Sortino — the two largest fitness components (60% combined weight).

hypothesis: When RSI is oversold (<30), price is typically 0.3-0.8% below VWAP. With VWAP_NEAR_BAND_PCT=0.50%, a price 0.6% below VWAP adds ~20 pts to bear_score (VWAP opposing the RSI bull signal). Expanding the neutral band to 0.75% neutralizes the VWAP bear vote for signals where price is slightly below VWAP, making RSI-extreme signals cleaner. These moderate-deviation setups (price just slightly below VWAP) are actually good mean-reversion candidates — not too extended. More quality signals pass MIN_CONFIDENCE=58 and those already passing do so with higher confidence, improving win rate and Sharpe.

hypothesis: RSI<25 signals in the first 15 minutes (9:30-9:45 ET) are often continuation-of-overnight-gap moves in early price discovery rather than true exhaustion reversals. Stocks with RSI<25 at 9:35 are still under active selling pressure. Extending the open filter from 5→15 minutes removes these lower-quality early-morning signals, leaving only mid-to-late-morning RSI extremes where selling has genuinely exhausted. Fewer but cleaner signals should improve win rate and Sharpe (the top fitness component at 35% weight).

When RSI<25 (extreme oversold), price is almost always below VWAP — yet the current VWAP logic adds that to bear_score, OPPOSING the RSI long signal. This splits confidence and causes valid setups to fail MIN_CONFIDENCE=58. Flipping VWAP to be bull-confirming when RSI<25+below VWAP (and bear-confirming when RSI>75+above VWAP) aligns both indicators, unlocks currently-filtered genuine extreme setups, and should increase trade count and Sharpe.

hypothesis: Current fitness=7.5312 with 66.67% WR suggests RSI scoring gap is limiting signal quality. RSI<25 gets 100% score (35 pts) but RSI 25-44 only gets 60% score (21 pts). In volatile markets (regime_mult=0.65), RSI 25-32 represents quality oversold mean-reversion opportunities but scores too low to pass MIN_CONFIDENCE=58.0. Adding RSI_STRONG_OVERSOLD=32 tier (80% score) bridges the gap, capturing more quality signals in 25-32 range without RSI<23's trade count risk.

hypothesis: Current fitness=8.1367 with 71.43% WR and 14 trades shows good signal quality but could benefit from higher trade frequency. Power hour (3-4 PM) has high volume/volatility with clear directional moves as institutions position for overnight. Current MARKET_CLOSE_CUTOFF=3:45 PM misses the final 15 minutes of active trading. Extending to 3:50 PM captures additional high-quality late-day signals (potentially 15-17 trades vs 14) while maintaining proper risk management before close. Late-day signals often have good follow-through due to overnight positioning flows.
